question: Is iron deficiency common in picky eating children and how serious is it?
topic: Addresses underrecognition of a serious nutritional consequence of picky eating

sources.name: Complementary Feeding Interventions for Infants and Young Children Under Age 2: Scoping of Promising Interventions to Implement at the Community or State Level

discovery_sources.text: Iron deficiency is the most common nutrient deficiency in children worldwide - should verify against WHO/global health data
discovery_sources.origin: Unbiased Science: The Joy of Cooking (For Kids Who Are ‘Picky’ Eaters)

discovery_sources.text: Iron deficiency anemia in picky eaters
